text.title: api-testing Failure Modes
text.summary: Migrated antipatterns for api-testing as failure modes. Add detector + repair clauses where missing.

failure-mode.description: Asserting only status code: assert response.status_code == 200
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Does not catch body schema drift, missing required fields, or wrong types. Add validate_response(spec, response) and at least one body field assertion.

failure-mode.description: Hardcoded primary keys like user_id="1" shared across test functions
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Collides across parallel test runs and masks order-dependent bugs. Use factories that generate unique values.

failure-mode.description: Agent-generated tests pile up time.sleep(1) to wait for async operations
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Creates slow, flaky tests. Replace with explicit polling helpers or pytest-asyncio fixtures.

failure-mode.description: Agent fixing a flaky test by adding --reruns without a root-cause fix
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Hides the real problem. Require any --reruns > 0 to have an issue link explaining the flake.

failure-mode.description: Agent commits production secrets into test config files
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Test files often skip linting. Run secret-scan on tests/ too.

failure-mode.description: E2E agent suite against shared staging mutates data for other tests
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Use per-run namespacing (org_id derived from PR number) or ephemeral environments.
